themes.name: Sun-protection myths & fear-mongering
themes.receipt: Diaper-cream trend (15.9M source + 2 derm reacts) · 'habits > products' 120× · hormone scare 865-comment fight

videos.author: beautydermmd
videos.caption: Not her serum. Not her skincare. HER HABITS. 👀 Sun protection is elite behavior. UPF clothing. Wide-brim hat. Sunglasses. Shade. This is what dermatologists notice first. You should too. ☀️ #dermatolo

videos.author: drdrayzday
videos.caption: Diaper rash cream doesn’t not work as sunscreen. #sunscreen #diaperrashcream #sunprotection @rob.lea

videos.author: drspf
videos.caption: UV index is not the stock market 📈📉 I am all for getting outside, being active, playing sports, and enjoying the sun safely…but intentionally sitting out to tan is not it. A tan is your skin’s respons

her_coverage.summary: Bemotrizinol/European-filters post 6 days ago (307K), UV-index reminder 9 days ago (169K), plus two long-form sunscreen guides in corpus.

themes.corpus_position: I think the reason that dermatologists take sun protection so seriously is we see the damage that it can do.

stitch_shortlist.author: rob.lea1
stitch_shortlist.claim: diaper cream + Zinka + Bag Balm survived an 11-hour ocean swim

pitch.the_claim: An open-ocean marathon swimmer credits diaper rash cream, Zinka, and Bag Balm for surviving 11+ hours of sun exposure.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 15.9M views at 3.6 days and the wave is carrying derm reacts already (Dr. Dray rode it on both platforms). Half-right is the best trigger: zinc oxide IS the real active — the packaging isn't the point.
pitch.your_fit: Her mineral-sunscreen corpus (zinc oxide guidance in both long-form sunscreen guides) + occlusives expertise from the slugging duet. Dr. Dray took the 'it works' lane — Abby's NEW angle: the zinc-percentage math and what Bag Balm actually does.
angles: empathy-first: why it DID work for 11 hours
zinc math: diaper cream % vs your SPF 50
Bag Balm is occlusion, not protection — know the difference

stitch_shortlist.author: robertwblove
stitch_shortlist.claim: avoid these sunscreens — they harm your hormones (aimed at parents)

pitch.the_claim: Chemical sunscreens 'harm your hormones' and parents should avoid them on children.
pitch.why_it_stitches: Classic filter fear-mongering in her exact lane with an 865-comment fight at 2.4 days — controversy fuel, correctable without punching down (worried parents deserve empathy, the claim doesn't).
pitch.your_fit: She posted the bemotrizinol/European-filters explainer 6 days ago (307K) — the perfect 'here's the actual fix if filters worry you' receipt, plus two long-form sunscreen guides in corpus.
angles: 'you want me to skip SPF over WHAT?'
what the hormone studies actually measured — dose matters

stitch_shortlist.author: meganmaceyy
stitch_shortlist.claim: your scalp burns fast, especially along your parting — sprays and powders exist

pitch.the_claim: The scalp (especially the part line) is the most-forgotten, fastest-burning sun spot; scalp sprays and powders solve it.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 385K in 2 days on a 7-second clip — and it's HER underexploited high-ceiling lane (scalp/hair). Mostly right, so this is a 'yes, and —' extension, not a correction.
pitch.your_fit: Strong sunscreen corpus; as a Mohs surgeon she operates on scalp skin cancers — the authority extension the original can't make. Product-specific picks need her take.
angles: yes, and — part-line skin cancer is real (Mohs cases)
powder vs spray vs hat: derm ranking
the spots everyone forgets: part, ears, lips

stitch_shortlist.author: shougmansour30
stitch_shortlist.claim: retinal serum on hands at night, sunscreen every next morning

pitch.the_claim: Nightly retinal serum on the backs of the hands (plus morning SPF) as an anti-aging hack.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 623K at 2.1 days, a genuinely good habit going viral — 'yes, and' it with derm precision (strength, frequency, the non-negotiable SPF pairing).
pitch.your_fit: Her retinoid-frequency position is corpus-verbatim ('Tretinoin can be used daily as tolerated; AHA/BHAs 1–3 times/week') from her own 545K post. Hand-specific strengths need her take.
angles: yes, and — hand anti-aging the derm way
which retinoid strength for hands vs face
why the morning-SPF rule is non-negotiable with retinoids

stitch_shortlist.author: dbeauty_glow
stitch_shortlist.claim: creators oil their skin to fake results before advertising products

pitch.the_claim: Skincare advertisers rub oil on their skin to fake 'results' before filming product promos.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 568K with a 1,264-comment pile-on at 2.1 days. Punches at an industry practice, not a person — safe and satisfying to extend.
pitch.your_fit: Her bad-better-best ratings content (116K this week) is exactly this skepticism lane, and today's glass-skin superviral gives her the 'what real glow requires' receipt.
angles: how to spot faked 'results' in 5 seconds
what glass skin ACTUALLY requires (my routine)
